# opnsense_dnsmasq_boot (Resource)

Manages a Dnsmasq DHCP boot entry on OPNsense. Boot entries are commonly used for PXE or network boot configuration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_dnsmasq_boot" "pxe" {
  filename    = "pxelinux.0"
  server_name = "pxe"
  address     = "192.0.2.20"
  description = "PXE boot entry"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `filename` (String) Boot filename served to matching clients.

### Optional

- `address` (String) Boot server address.
- `description` (String) Description for this DHCP boot entry.
- `interface` (String) Interface this boot entry applies to; blank means any allowed interface.
- `server_name` (String) Boot server name.
- `tag` (Set of String) Tags this boot entry matches.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier.

## Import

Import is supported using the Dnsmasq DHCP boot UUID from OPNsense.

```shell
terraform import opnsense_dnsmasq_boot.pxe 12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012
```
